pr.title: Fix missing dependencies in depfiles, refactor/simplify configure.py
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Fixed build dependency tracking by removing the split between regular and system include paths in configure.py: project and SDK headers are now passed as regular includes so they appear in depfiles. The PR also simplified DolphinLib configuration by reusing cflags_base, removed the DolphinLib extern mode and its call-site flag noise, and adjusted a small amount of extern/dolphin source to stop depending on local Runtime/platform.h helper macros, making the Dolphin SDK code easier to upstream.
what_changed: Removed system_includes_base and the Lib(..., system_includes=...) parameter path from configure.py.
Stopped passing project and SDK headers through the compiler's system include mechanism with -I- plus separate system include paths.
Kept include directories in includes_base as regular includes so included headers are tracked in generated depfiles.
Removed build/{version}/include from cflags_base/include handling in the shown diff.

Simplified DolphinLib by deleting the extern boolean mode and making Dolphin libraries consistently use cflags_base plus Dolphin-specific flags: -requireprotos, -fp_contract off, and -ir extern/dolphin/src.
Set DolphinLib src_dir to extern/dolphin/src and its include list to extern/dolphin/include, extern/dolphin/include/libc, and src/MSL.
Removed extern=True arguments from many DolphinLib declarations for amcstubs, OdemuExi2, hio, mcc, thp, base, db, dsp, dvd, gx, mtx, os, pad, vi, ai, ar, card, ax, and axfx libraries.
Removed includes of platform.h from extern/dolphin/src/dolphin/os/init/__start.c and extern/dolphin/src/dolphin/pad/pad.c.

Replaced Runtime/platform.h helper macros in extern/dolphin sources with compiler-native syntax: AT_ADDRESS declarations became colon-address declarations, SECTION_INIT became __declspec(section ".init"), WEAK became __declspec(weak), and false became FALSE in the shown OSResetSystem call.
smart_moves: The build fix addresses the actual depfile blind spot: headers included via system include paths were not appearing as dependencies, so moving local code and SDK headers to regular include paths makes incremental rebuilds more reliable.
The refactor reduces configuration branching by making DolphinLib one consistent code path instead of switching on an extern parameter at every call site.
Reusing cflags_base avoids duplicating long compiler flag lists for external Dolphin objects, lowering the chance that future flag tweaks diverge between game, SDK, and external library builds.

Removing Runtime/platform.h dependencies from extern/dolphin code improves upstreamability by expressing address placement, weak linkage, and section placement with source-level compiler syntax rather than melee-local wrapper macros.
decomp_lessons: Do not put repository-owned headers behind system include flags if depfiles are expected to drive correct incremental rebuilds; system includes may be omitted from dependency output.
Prefer a single shared base flag list for related Metrowerks targets, then layer only the small target-specific differences on top.
External SDK source is easier to compare, share, and upstream when it does not include project-specific convenience headers such as Runtime/platform.h.

When removing local portability macros from external code, preserve semantics explicitly: address-bound globals can use colon address syntax, init-section functions can use __declspec(section ".init"), and weak functions can use __declspec(weak).
Build-system cleanup can be matching-relevant even when object code changes are small, because include ordering and compiler flags affect reproducibility and dependency correctness.
naming_conventions: The configure.py helper naming retained Lib, DolphinLib, SysdolphinLib, MeleeLib, MatchingFor, Object, Matching, and NonMatching.
The refactor removed the DolphinLib extern keyword argument rather than renaming it, indicating the concept became unnecessary.
External Dolphin SDK symbols kept their upstream-style names in the diff, including Pad3Button, __OSWirelessPadFixMode, __check_pad3, __start, __init_registers, and __init_data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: DolphinLib now applies -fp_contract off and -ir extern/dolphin/src alongside cflags_base for Dolphin SDK compilation.
The OS init assembly function __start kept its asm body and init-section placement while replacing SECTION_INIT WEAK with __declspec(section ".init") __declspec(weak).
Address-pinned globals in external Dolphin source were kept without platform.h by using declarations such as u16 Pad3Button : PAD3_BUTTON_ADDR and u16 __OSWirelessPadFixMode : 0x800030E0.
The PR removed an explicit -inline auto from part of the base flag setup shown in the diff, but the context does not state a matching rationale beyond the configure.py simplification.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.

The PR body stated the rationale: headers included as system includes are not included in depfiles, regular includes should be used for project code similar to upstream dtk-template, DolphinLib can reuse cflags_base, and Runtime includes should be removed from extern/dolphin code to ease upstreaming.
follow_up_queries: Find later configure.py changes touching includes_base or system include behavior to see whether depfile handling changed again.
Search for remaining Runtime/platform.h includes under extern/dolphin to identify other upstreamability cleanup candidates.
Search for remaining AT_ADDRESS, SECTION_INIT, or WEAK macros in extern/dolphin sources that could be converted to compiler-native syntax.
Check whether removal of -inline auto or build/{version}/include had any follow-up matching or build fixes.
